stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east to New England -- and his stock's gushin'
stories.read: Well pardner, the Eagles finally shipped A.J. Brown to the Patriots, and here's the kicker -- this ain't a downgrade. Young Drake Maye led the whole frontier in completion rate and yards-per-throw last fall, slingin' the deep ball clean while Hurts was overthrowin' the canyon. Brown stays a top-shelf WR1 with a richer share of the throws in New England. The bonus strike? Back in Philly, DeVonta Smith just inherited a fatter cut of the targets -- go pan that creek before your outfit wakes up.

stories.headline: Kyler Murray takes Minnesota's reins -- a cheap horse with legs
stories.read: Hold up there -- the word out of the Vikings camp is the QB race ain't close, with Kyler Murray well saddled to start over young J.J. McCarthy. That's a vein worth pannin'. Murray's run for 400-plus yards five times, and those legs are free fantasy points no arm can match. Best part? The saloon's still grabbin' him in the late rounds, so you stake a startin' quarterback for pennies. Toss in a healthy Justin Jefferson catchin' from him and you've got cheap gold, pardner.

stories.headline: Myles Garrett rides to the Rams -- their defense is about to flood
stories.read: Straight off the wire: the Rams pulled the splash of the offseason, haulin' two-time Defensive Player of the Year Myles Garrett out of Cleveland. Pair him with Byron Young and his dozen sacks, and L.A. has the meanest pass-rush punch on the trail -- quarterbacks won't have time to spit. That makes the Rams DST a top-two claim come the great land grab. Now here's Doc's whisper: don't reach early for a defense. Let the herd overpay -- you rent a cheaper horse Week 1 and trade up when the showdowns line up right.
